title: Level 215
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 215: One Yard Away from Shore}}__NOTOC__


[[File:Seashell.png|300px]]

If my dream offers a more comfortable experience than reality,
why shouldn't I choose to forever drown within?


[[File:Meglogo-black-and-white.png|200px]]
'''Level 215''', colloquially known as '''"One Yard Away from Shore,"'''
is the 216th level of the Backrooms.

Discovered in June 23rd, 1529, 02:15:31.

== Description == {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Path of convex mirror.png|caption=A clear photograph that shows a straight path of the route.}} One who enters the level will begin on a route that starts out as a straight, seemingly limitless path covered uniformly with grey bricks, flanked by towering evergreen trees on either side that creates dappled shadows on the ground. Occasional convex mirrors are mounted and positioned periodically along the route, adding to the disorienting effect as they reflects the continuous stretch of the path itself. The architecture of the area is defined by its repetitive style, with the route extending infinitely ahead amidst a rather cool and shadowed atmosphere.

The impression of solitude is heightened by the muted lighting and still air that permeate through the dense canopy of evergreens in the level, accompanied by an eerie silence that occasionally interrupted by the sounds of rustling leaves, and thus amplifying the sense of isolation. The neat, monotony and uniformity of the surroundings makes it difficult for explorers to maintain their spatial awareness, which may lead to mental exhaustion and bewilderment. Explorers are advised to use breadcrumb trails or digital mapping devices to track movement and maintain communication with a team to mitigate any risks.

As one progresses forward and deeper through the passage, a gradual shift in weather conditions will happen, which starts as the lines of dense evergreen trees begins to thin out, offering glimpses of distant ocean waves and the mellow sound of surf crashing against the coast. This phenomenon indicates that one is getting close to the transition zone, where they would notice or experience a subtle shift in ambiance that blurs the junction of the enclosed route and the expansive beach. Atmospheric shifts include the gradual increase in sunlight intensity, temperature and humidity together with 10-15 knot average wind speeds.

After the transition, one will reach an area that depicts an expansive and unspoiled beach, with fine, undisturbed sand that stretches along the shoreline. Weathered lifeguard chairs and white barrels punctuates the beach that resembles remnants that suggests human intervention. The weather is typically stable here, with occasional shifts in wind direction and intensity. However, they don't really do any harm as they are mostly gentle breezes rather than strong winds. The water's surface appears calm and tranquil, with gentle undulations caused by the movement of waves against the shoreline. The height of the tides varies slightly but noticeably influenced by understated changes in wind direction and tidal patterns, with high tides reaching an average height of 1.5 meters above the mean sea level. {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Seashore.png|caption=A picture of the shoreline.}} This moderate elevation often transforms the coastal landscape, submerging portions of the beach under a blanket of seawater while revealing expansive stretches of sand during low tides. The impact of these tidal shifts alters and reshapes the terrain near the shoreline. The size of waves generated by tidal movements ranges from gentle undulations to occasional swells due to its underwater topography. Waves typically travel at speeds ranging from 5 to 15 knots, and follows a consistent yet surreal pattern, affected by unknown forces rather than lunar cycles or gravitational pulls. The tidal movements occur approximately every 6 hours and 12 minutes. For a small chance, explorers might observe occasional whitecaps forming on the crests of larger waves. Underneath the surface, the water maintains a clear and crystalline quality, allowing visibility to a certain depth.

The sand in the area is predominantly composed of fine grains that are soft and smooth to the touch. Each particle is uniformly sized, creating a smooth, velvety texture underfoot. The color of the sand varies lightly, ranging from pale ivory to light beige under the sunlight. Occasional gentle slopes may form near the water's edge, shaped by the ebb and flow of tidal movements and sometimes affected by the indirect influence of wind patterns. Despite its apparent stability, the sand remains responsive to environmental factors, finely shifting and resettling over time. == Entities == '''Level 215''' is characterized by a profound emptiness, devoid of any typical entities. This absence heightens the spooky and surreal feeling of the level by fostering a sense of isolation and stillness. We've received many reports of a possibility entity about a little girl that is not a part of the [[Entity 9|Facelings]], but we have no evidence to prove its presence. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the expansive terrain and unique properties of '''Level 215''', no establishments or permanent housing can be made. == Entrances and Exits == ===Entrances=== *Diving into any clear waterbodies in [[Level 182]] has a chance to reach here. ===Exits=== *Walking into the ocean until you reach an unusual wooden door. Entering it will lead one to [[Level 216]]
"In this endless passage, where sorrow meets the tranquil shore."
"I linger in the whispers of the waves,
forever seeking the peace that was stolen from me."


'''I'''t hurts... everything hurts.
One moment, I was just walking, and then... the car came out of nowhere.
Why didn't he see me? I don't understand.
I can't move my legs. They feel so heavy, and my head is spinning.

'''W'''here am I? This place was already so bad, and now... now it's worse.
I thought I heard the sound of the ocean earlier.
I always loved the beach. Mom and Dad would take me there in the summer.
The sound of the waves, the feeling of the sand under my feet... it made me so happy.
But this place... it's so different. So empty. So horrible.

'''H'''e's coming towards me now.
I can see the fear in his eyes.
Why is he so scared? I'm the one who's hurt.
Maybe he's going to help me. Please, help me...

'''B'''ut wait... what is he doing?
No, don't pick me up! It hurts too much.
Please, be careful.
Where are you taking me? The beach...
I can hear the waves louder now. It's getting closer..

''(splash)''


[[File:Seasa.png|500px]]


'''I''''m so tired. Everything is fading.
If I close my eyes, will I wake up back at home?
I just want to go home... I can't breathe..
The water is so cold. It's pulling me away.
Why would he do this?

'''I''' don't want to die... not like this.
I want to see my family again.
I want to play on the beach and feel the sun on my face.
But now, it's all slipping away...

'''M'''aybe, if I stay here... in this place...
The calming wind and trees... I might forget the pain I felt.
The endless routes... the tranquil shore... this is my home now. Forever.


[[File:Lantern.png|200px]]

Oh, hello. You're another new face. Nice to meet you here.
Here, I'll guide you across the shore.
Let my story be a lantern that illuminates every ripple of the sea.

And now, it's time to say goodbye.


[[File:Hearts.png|100px]]
